nodes.label: GCE VM + docker compose Deployment (per environment)
nodes.rationale: One e2-small VM per environment runs timescaledb + one-shot migrate + api over IAP-tunneled SSH; .env is rendered per-deploy from GitHub Environment vars/secrets, chmod 600, deleted after up -d api — no long-lived public SSH and no secrets baked into images. Supersedes the earlier Cloud Run design.

nodes.label: Workload Identity Federation for CI Auth
nodes.rationale: GitHub Actions impersonates the deployer service account via short-lived WIF tokens instead of a downloaded key file, so there is no long-lived credential to rotate, leak, or commit.

nodes.label: Digest-pinned Production Promotion
nodes.rationale: Production deploys resolve the release commit to immutable image digests and never rebuild, closing the gap where a mutable tag could be repointed at an uninspected build; integration deploys by tag since it builds the image itself in the same run.

nodes.label: Migration-before-deploy + Expand/Contract Invariant
nodes.rationale: The migration runs to completion before the api container restarts, so the previous revision briefly serves the migrated schema — every migration must be backward-compatible: additive expand changes ship alone, contract changes land only after a revision no longer depending on the old shape is stable.

nodes.label: Per-environment JWT Signing Keys
nodes.rationale: Integration and production hold distinct RSA keys so staging can never mint a token production would accept — a shared key would let a lower-trust environment's token authenticate against production.

nodes.label: TimescaleDB Extension Gap: Resolved by In-stack Database
nodes.rationale: Cloud SQL/Neon/Supabase lack TimescaleDB support needed by the continuous aggregates, so running the same TimescaleDB image in the compose stack guarantees the extension by construction — accepting ownership of VM patching, uptime, and backups in exchange.
